description: Add YouTube video embeds to the docs index page and polish the voice to closer match the README's first-person personality. Two videos from the README (main demo, permission dialog demo) need a reusable YouTube embed component and placement on the index page. The index page voice needs a pass to fix 'We' → 'I' and add more of the README's genuine personality.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Create a reusable YouTube embed component for MDX pages
userStories.description: Create a YouTubeEmbed component styled to match the docs neo-brutalist design system. Register it in MDX.tsx so it can be used as <YouTubeEmbed /> in any .mdx file.
acceptanceCriteria: A YouTubeEmbed component exists that accepts videoId and title props
The component renders a responsive 16:9 iframe with youtube-nocookie.com embed URL
The component is styled with the docs design tokens — border-2 border-border, shadow-md, no border-radius (matching --radius: 0)
The component is registered in docComponents in MDX.tsx and usable from .mdx files as <YouTubeEmbed />
The component uses loading='lazy' on the iframe for performance
userStories.notes: Files: - docs/components/YouTubeEmbed.tsx (new file — the component) - docs/components/MDX.tsx (register component in docComponents) Context: The docs site uses a neo-brutalist design system. Key tokens from docs/app/global.css: - --radius: 0 (no border-radius) - --shadow-md: 4px 4px 0 0 var(--border) (flat offset shadow) - --border: #000 (light) / #777777 (dark) - border-2 border-border is the standard border style The component should be a simple wrapper around a YouTube iframe. Use youtube-nocookie.com for privacy. Props: videoId (string, required), title (string, required for accessibility). Styling approach: ```tsx

userStories.title: Add YouTube videos to docs index page and polish voice to match README personality
userStories.description: Add both YouTube demo videos from the README to the docs index page in natural positions. Fix the remaining voice issues — 'We reverse-engineered' should be 'I reverse-engineered' with the README's parenthetical humor, and the overall tone should feel like the same person who wrote the README.
acceptanceCriteria: The main demo video (PBvUXDAGVM8) appears after the intro paragraphs and before 'Who Is This For?' — it's the first visual on the page
The security/permissions demo video (6CL6kwk8d9w) appears in or near the 'A Note on Trust' section
The intro paragraph uses first person singular — 'I reverse-engineered' not 'We reverse-engineered'
The intro has the README's parenthetical humor energy — the '(okay, I asked Claude Opus to reverse-engineer)' vibe, adapted naturally for the docs context
All existing MDX components (<HowItWorks />) and links preserved exactly
The page structure and sections remain the same — only prose and video placement change
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/index.mdx (voice polish + video placement)

Context:
The README (repo root README.md) is the voice reference. The docs index page was recently reworked to match the README voice, but some issues remain:

1. Line 9 says "We reverse-engineered" — should be "I reverse-engineered". The README uses first person singular throughout. The docs can be slightly different in tone (it's a marketing/landing page, not a README) but the pronoun should be consistent with the README's personal voice.

2. The README has this brilliant parenthetical: "I reverse-engineered (okay, I asked Claude Opus to reverse-engineer) the internal APIs..." — the docs should have similar energy. It doesn't need to be identical, but it should feel like the same person wrote it.

Current index.mdx (57 lines):
```
---
title: Introduction
description: OpenTabs gives AI agents access to any web application through your authenticated browser session.
lastUpdated: 2026-02-28
---

You're already logged into dozens of web apps in Chrome. Why should your AI need a separate set of credentials?

OpenTabs gives your AI agent access to web applications through your existing browser sessions. We reverse-engineered the internal APIs that web apps use — the same endpoints their own frontend calls — and exposed them as MCP tools your AI agent can call directly. No API keys. No OAuth. No service accounts. If you can see it in a tab, your agent can use it.

No screenshots. No DOM scraping. No pixel-guessing. When your AI wants to send a Slack message, it calls `slack_send_message` — the exact same API call Slack's own web app makes, running in your browser, using your existing session. It's fast, it's cheap on tokens (tokens are money), and the knowledge gets packaged into a reusable plugin anyone can install.

Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, [OpenCode](https://opencode.ai), and any MCP client that supports Streamable HTTP.

## Who Is This For?
...
```

Video placement:

1. Main demo video (PBvUXDAGVM8 — "AI checks stocks, orders food, and sends a Discord message") — place it after the intro paragraphs (after line 13 "Works with...") and before "## Who Is This For?". This mirrors the README where it appears right after the tagline. Use:
```
<YouTubeEmbed videoId="PBvUXDAGVM8" title="Demo: AI checks stocks, orders food, and sends a Discord message — all through the browser" />
```

2. Security demo video (6CL6kwk8d9w — "Permission dialog asking for approval before a tool call executes") — place it at the end of the "A Note on Trust" section (after line 53). This mirrors the README where it appears right after the Security section bullets. Use:
```
<YouTubeEmbed videoId="6CL6kwk8d9w" title="Demo: Permission dialog asking for approval before a tool call executes" />
```

Voice changes:
- Line 9: Change "We reverse-engineered" to "I reverse-engineered" with the parenthetical humor from the README
- Review all other prose for consistency — the rest is mostly good but make sure it feels like ONE person wrote it
- The page can be slightly more polished than the README (it IS the docs landing page) but should feel personal, genuine, and warm — not corporate

Constraints:
- Keep ALL existing sections: Who Is This For?, How It Works, What's Included, Key Features, A Note on Trust, Next Steps
- Keep <HowItWorks /> component exactly
- Keep all links exactly as they are
- Keep frontmatter structure
- Do NOT restructure sections or add new headings
- Do NOT copy the README verbatim — adapt the energy, don't duplicate the text
